us10y.description: United States 10 Year Government Bonds Yield

narrative: With the cash session behind us, index futures are still repricing what tomorrow might pay. Nasdaq and S&P futures sit lower overall — cautious tone: futures soft; focus on a+ setups with tight risk — so treat after-hours prints as liquidity-thin context unless a major catalyst hits.

The vol complex and the rate backdrop remain the cleanest risk tell. Vix is lower on the session read, while US10Y is higher. If both deteriorate together, assume wider ranges and slower fills until the tape proves otherwise.

What happened overseas still matters for how US names gap. Europe finished higher on balance. Asia lower into the US evening. Global sync: Europe strong but Asia weak — expect choppy US open and rotation risk.

Situational Awareness: Don’t overfit the close — carry forward the regime read (leadership, Vix, US10Y), then map the next set of catalysts (Fed speak/data/earnings, energy/FX) and define the tripwire that would invalidate your lean into the next open. Risk checks: globex gaps are common; confirm with breadth and liquidity on the next cash open, and treat sudden shifts in rates/vol as regime warnings.

Into the next session, favor a plan over a prediction: reduce size if structure is messy, stick with liquid A+ Stage-2 leaders from your theme work, and let the opening range validate (or invalidate) the globex lean.

bullets: A+ Stage-2 only; liquid tape; wait for cash confirmation if globex is noisy.
